turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey sophia how is Sophia doing today

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: Sophia has been better Sophia's primary care doctor wanted Sophia to see the doctor because of this knee pain that Sophia has been having for about six months now

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay does Sophia remember what caused the knee pain initially

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the patient knows the patient really ca n't recall any specific event that caused the knee pain

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright well can the doctor describe the knee pain for the patient

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah so the knee pain is it's like a deep achy pain that the patient feels like the knee pain is behind Sophia's kneecaps and sometimes Sophia can even hear like what seems like like some creaking in Sophia's knees

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hmmm alright now tell the doctor what what type of activities makes the knee pain worse

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: so Sophia feels like the knee pain gets worse anytime Sophia is like getting from like a seated position to standing so Sophia works from home so Sophia is at Sophia's desk a lot so anytime Sophia gets up Sophia has some knee pain or after watching tv or even like going up and down stairs the activity of going up and down stairs is really painful

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hmmm okay and does anything make the knee pain feel better

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: usually after Sophia rests the knee for some time resting the knee does help with the knee pain

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay now Sophia did mention earlier that Sophia has tried some things in the past can Sophia tell the doctor what the things were

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah so when the knee first started hurting Sophia was putting ice on the knee and Sophia even got like some ace bandage like wrapping from the pharmacy Sophia does take ibuprofen or aleve sometimes but Sophia tries to avoid really taking any medications

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay and and tell the doctor what is Sophia's day like

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: well Sophia tries to be as active as Sophia can Sophia actually loves to run and Sophia would run like five to six miles a day but now Sophia i mean honestly Sophia ca n't even get to half a mile and Sophia's leg will start hurting Sophia's knee will start hurting

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay that's definitely a decrease in Sophia's mileage there where has Sophia been running lately like does Sophia run outside or on a treadmill or

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah Sophia you know Sophia will do the treadmill during the winter but Sophia just loves being outdoors and running

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah yeah there are some really nice senior routes here in boston is n't there like

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: there are

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so many knee places there's some right by the harbor that the doctor has i've done some runs there before too the harbor route is pretty nice well i mean the boston marathon what can the doctor say right

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: right exactly exactly no boston is very nice

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: well okay well hopefully that the doctor gets Sophia feeling better so Sophia can start running again more than . five miles but tell the doctor though has Sophia noticed any swelling or redness in Sophia's knees

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no not really

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay and has Sophia ever injured Sophia's knees before

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no Sophia has been pretty good Sophia actually has never broken a bone actually no Sophia is gon na change that actually Sophia did break Sophia's arm when Sophia was in sixth grade but nothing since then

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: Sophia

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay and it's just Sophia's arm right not not Sophia's knee

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: right

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay well alright well let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam on Sophia here let the doctor look at Sophia's vital signs okay the doctor reviewed Sophia's vitals Sophia's vitals overall Sophia's vitals look good the doctor will take a listen to Sophia's heart real quick alright and Sophia's heart exam the doctor hears regular rate and rhythm with no murmurs and on Sophia's musculoskeletal exam the doctor has seen Sophia and walk around here and Sophia does have normal gait alright now the doctor is gon na have Sophia do some movements on Sophia's legs here okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright good Sophia's strength is three out of five for abduction of legs bilaterally and the remainder of muscle strength for Sophia's lower extremities are five out of five so that's good let the doctor take a look at Sophia's knee now on Sophia's knee exam there is no overlying erythema that's redness or ecchymosis and that's bruising or any warmth of the skin appreciated there is no effusion let the doctor go ahead and feel around here does palpation hurt right here how does palpation feel

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: palpation does

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: sorry so there is tenderness to palpation of the bilateral retinacular retinaculum and there is positive patellar grind test alright and can Sophia are Sophia able to squat for the doctor

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: Sophia can but squatting hurts

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay alright so Sophia is definitely having some knee pain with squatting and the doctor is just gon na maneuver Sophia's leg just a little bit here real quick okay so lachman anterior and posterior drawer and mcmurray tests are all negative bilaterally so that's good there okay now Sophia's neuro exam patella and achilles reflexes are symmetrical alright so now the doctor did review the results of Sophia's x-ray and both of Sophia's knees which shows no fractures or osteoarthritis so let the doctor go ahead and tell Sophia about the doctor's assessment and plan so for Sophia's problem of bilateral knee pain based on what Sophia told the doctor Sophia's exam and reviewing Sophia's mri Sophia's symptoms are consistent with patellofemoral pain syndrome and patellofemoral pain syndrome is a very common condition that the doctor sees that causes knee pain especially in active young people so patellofemoral pain syndrome has to do with the way Sophia's kneecap which is Sophia's patella how Sophia's patella moves along the groove of Sophia's thigh Sophia's thigh bone which is the femur now for the knee pain Sophia may continue taking ibuprofen or anti-inflammatory as needed to help with the knee pain but the doctor is also going to recommend physical therapy where where the physical therapy team will show Sophia a number of lower extremity exercises and the lower extremity exercises will help increase Sophia's lower extremity strength Sophia's mobility and correct any incorrect running mechanics that Sophia may have so does Sophia have any questions for the doctor about the assessment and plan

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: will Sophia be able to run anytime soon

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: well returning to running is a goal of treatment and to get Sophia back in running again now Sophia will have to take an take it easy from running for now but the doctor will continue to assess Sophia's progress with each visit okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright anything else

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no Sophia thinks that's it

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay well very good well the doctor will be following up with Sophia soon and again just take it easy for now from the running okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay thank the doctor

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: bye

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: bye
